payload.hypothesis: Severe stagnation and two non-competitive K=128 architecture probes suggest the current global-ranking support mechanisms are locally exhausted. A new `group_topk` prototype that enforces one local winner per small latent group before the global top-k may reduce redundant feature selection and improve reconstruction quality or cost under the same validated training regime.

After nine non-improving rounds and non-competitive K=128 results from topk and routed, the current global support-ranking direction looks structurally weak. The newly added `group_topk` family is the next fundamentally different architecture probe because it imposes local winner-take-all competition before global selection, which may improve feature diversity and reduce redundant support allocation under the same validated training regime.

In crash recovery mode, the safest informative move is to return to the best known working gated K=128 configuration and make the smallest remaining stabilization adjustment. A milder auxiliary pressure of AUXK_ALPHA=0.01 may preserve the strong gated baseline while avoiding the degradation seen at 0.0625, giving a cleaner read on whether the current anchor is slightly over-regularized.

With the gated family now improved at K=128 via AUXK_ALPHA=0.01, the next clean move is to test whether that stronger regime transfers to a smaller sparsity budget and improves the Pareto frontier rather than only the anchor point. A gated K=64 proxy run under the same stabilized settings can reveal whether the current best family can materially outperform the old JumpReLU K=64 point.

The stabilized gated regime has now improved both K=128 and K=64, so the next clean Pareto probe is to test whether that same family extends to K=32 and can displace the older JumpReLU low-K point. A gated K=32 proxy run under the same validated settings will show whether the current best family can dominate more of the frontier rather than remaining a high-K-only solution.

Now that gated holds the full frontier at K=128, K=64, and K=32, the next informative move is to test whether the low-K regime is over-capacitated rather than quality-limited. Reducing gated expansion_factor from 16 to 8 at K=32 may preserve most of the newly won quality while improving cost signals and possibly sharpening feature competition enough to strengthen the Pareto tradeoff.

The last round showed the gated low-K capacity cut was a real quality regression, and the search has now gone several rounds without advancing an incubating alternative family. The least-bad informative move is to get a real proxy read on the already-implemented `group_topk` family at K=128 under the strongest current training regime, removing the unsupported `GROUP_TOPK_SIZE` override that caused the earlier policy crashes. This will finally test whether local winner-take-all competition can beat other non-gated support selectors on anchor-quality rather than being discarded for an execution-layer mismatch.
